description: Tests that blacklisting is PROJECT-scoped, not pipeline-scoped. If project A blacklists a company, project B can still contact them. Also tests that within the SAME project, dedup prevents re-gathering already-known companies.

project_b.name: TestProjectB
project_b.note: Different project, should NOT see Result's blacklist

steps.description: Verify Result project has 215 companies from previous tests
steps.user_prompt: How many companies do we have in the Result project?

steps.description: Create a separate project to test blacklist isolation
steps.user_prompt: Create a new project called TestProjectB targeting IT consulting in London

steps.description: Gather for project B — should NOT be affected by Result's companies
steps.user_prompt: Gather companies for TestProjectB from this CSV: /data/test_overlap.csv
steps.critical_requirement: Even though Result project has 215 companies, TestProjectB should see ALL companies as NEW. Blacklisting and dedup are per-project.

steps.description: Verify the two projects have independent blacklists
Now gather the SAME CSV again for Result — should be 100% duplicates
steps.user_prompt: Add the CSV data again to Result project: /data/take-test-100.csv
steps.critical_requirement: Re-gathering the same CSV for the same project should yield 0 new companies. ALL are duplicates because they were discovered in the first CSV run.

db_checks: discovered_companies for Result: exactly 215 unique domains (unchanged)
discovered_companies for TestProjectB: independent set, no contamination from Result
No domain appears in both Result and TestProjectB discovered_companies unless intentionally gathered for both
Re-gathering CSV for Result created 0 new DiscoveredCompany records (all deduped)
rules_verified: Blacklist is PROJECT-scoped: different projects can contact same company
Dedup is PROJECT-scoped: same domain in same project = duplicate
Re-gathering same source for same project = 0 new companies
